steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $f(x)=36(6,561)^{\frac{x}{2}}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Every increase of 2 in $a$ multiplies $f(a)$ by the constant $c$. All four choices are equivalent forms of the same function; verify with the remaining alternative: $f(x)=36(81)^{x}$. When $x$ increases by 2, $f(x+2)=36(81)^{x+2}=36(81)^x \cdot 81^2 = f(x)\cdot 6561$. So $c=6561$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The question asks which form displays $c$ as the base or coefficient. Since $c=6561$, we need a form where 6561 appears literally as the base of the exponential or as a coefficient.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Check each choice for whether its base or coefficient equals 6561:
  • A: $f(x)=36(3)^{4x}$ — base is 3, not 6561. When $x$ increases by 2, factor is $3^{8}=6561$, but 6561 is not visible as the base or coefficient. Eliminated.
  • B: $f(x)=36(9)^{2x}$ — base is 9, not 6561. Factor per $+2$ is $9^4=6561$, but 6561 is not displayed. Eliminated.
  • $f(x)=36(81)^{x}$: $f(x)=36(81)^x$ — base is 81, not 6561. Factor per $+2$ is $81^2=6561$, but 6561 is not displayed. Eliminated.
  • $f(x)=36(6,561)^{\frac{x}{2}}$: $f(x)=36(6561)^{\frac{x}{2}}$ — base is 6561. When $x$ increases by 2, $\frac{x}{2}$ increases by 1, so $f$ multiplies by $6561^1=6561=c$. The value of $c$ is the base itself. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
